name: xlsx-for-ai
description: Reliable Excel/.xlsx access for AI agents. Read, write, diff, redact, validate, describe, filter, aggregate — backed by a hosted API. Free tier auto-registers anonymously on first use.
long_description:
**xlsx-for-ai** is the missing reliability layer that makes spreadsheet reasoning production-grade for LLM agents.

Unlike pandas-style sandboxes, it preserves merged cells, named ranges, formulas, conditional formatting, charts, pivots, and slicers. Cross-engine validation catches the corruption pandas silently masks.

- **Free tier (10,000 calls/month)** — auto-registers an anonymous client_id on first use. No email, no signup form.
- **Paid tiers** (Bronze / Silver / Gold) for commercial use.
- **Server-mediated** — engine runs on api.xlsx-for-ai.dev, not your machine. The thin client only reads local file paths and relays bytes.
- **Dynamic tool catalog** — the tool surface is discovered from the server at startup. New tools appear without re-installing the extension.

tools.name: xlsx_read
tools.description: Read an .xlsx file and return its contents as text/JSON/markdown.

tools.name: xlsx_list_sheets
tools.description: List sheets in an .xlsx workbook with row/column counts.

tools.name: xlsx_schema
tools.description: Infer column types from the first N rows with confidence scoring.

tools.name: xlsx_diff
tools.description: Compare two .xlsx workbooks and report cell-level differences (deterministic, hashable).

tools.name: xlsx_write
tools.description: Generate or edit an .xlsx file from a JSON spec.

tools.name: xlsx_redact
tools.description: Strip cell values while preserving structure (formulas, named ranges, formatting).

tools.name: xlsx_describe
tools.description: Pandas-style per-column summary stats (count, nulls, unique, min/max/mean/std).

tools.name: xlsx_filter
tools.description: Predicate-based row filter with eq/ne/gt/gte/lt/lte/contains/in/is_null operators.

tools.name: xlsx_aggregate
tools.description: GroupBy + aggregate (sum/mean/min/max/count/count_distinct).

tools.name: xlsx_sort
tools.description: Multi-column sort with per-column direction and stable ordering.

tools.name: xlsx_value_counts
tools.description: Frequency counts for a column, with optional top_n and null handling.

tools.name: xlsx_pivot
tools.description: Pivot table with index/columns/values + configurable aggregation.

tools.name: xlsx_named_ranges
tools.description: List defined names in the workbook with scope and reference.

tools.name: xlsx_formulas
tools.description: Inventory and inspect formulas across sheets, optionally with results.

tools.name: xlsx_eval
tools.description: Evaluate ad-hoc formulas against the workbook context.

tools.name: xlsx_tables
tools.description: List Excel Tables with column metadata.

tools.name: xlsx_convert
tools.description: Convert .xlsx to other formats (CSV, JSON, etc).

tools.name: xlsx_validate
tools.description: Cross-engine consistency check across two independent xlsx renderers.

tools.name: xlsx_data_validations
tools.description: Inspect cell data validation rules (dropdowns, ranges, custom rules).

tools.name: xlsx_hyperlinks
tools.description: Inventory hyperlinks across the workbook.

tools.name: xlsx_topology
tools.description: Map cross-sheet references and dependency structure.

tools.name: xlsx_conditional_formats
tools.description: Inspect conditional formatting rules per range.

tools.name: xlsx_comments
tools.description: Read cell comments and threaded discussions.

tools.name: xlsx_doctor
tools.description: Audit workbook health — surface issues, brittle formulas, broken references.

tools.name: xlsx_form_controls
tools.description: Inspect form controls (buttons, checkboxes, dropdowns) and their bindings.

tools.name: xlsx_macros
tools.description: Detect and inspect VBA macros and their entry points.

tools.name: xlsx_merged_cells
tools.description: Inventory merged cell regions per sheet.

tools.name: xlsx_workbook_views
tools.description: Inspect saved views, frozen panes, zoom levels.

tools.name: xlsx_print_settings
tools.description: Inspect print areas, page breaks, headers/footers.

tools.name: xlsx_properties
tools.description: Read workbook document properties (author, created, custom properties).

tools.name: xlsx_external_links
tools.description: List references to external workbooks and their resolution status.

tools.name: xlsx_slicers_timelines
tools.description: Inspect slicers and timelines linked to pivot tables.

tools.name: xlsx_pivot_tables
tools.description: Inspect pivot table structure, fields, and source data.

tools.name: xlsx_images
tools.description: List embedded images with anchor and dimensions.

tools.name: xlsx_charts
tools.description: Inspect charts — types, data ranges, series.

tools.name: xlsx_protection
tools.description: Inspect sheet/workbook protection settings.

tools.name: xlsx_styles
tools.description: Inspect cell styles, fonts, fills, and formatting metadata.

tools.name: xlsx_post_slack
tools.description: Post a workbook to a Slack channel as a file attachment. BYOA — pass the user's Slack bot token in the call; never persisted.

tools.name: xlsx_post_teams
tools.description: Post a workbook to a Microsoft Teams channel as a file attachment in a channel message. BYOA — pass the user's Microsoft Graph access token in the call; never persisted.

tools.name: xlsx_stamp
tools.description: Sign a workbook with a cryptographic integrity-verification stamp (factual checks + content hash, embedded in docProps/custom.xml). The recipient can verify the file hasn't been tampered with since.

tools.name: xlsx_verify_stamp
tools.description: Verify a workbook's embedded integrity-verification stamp. Returns whether the signature is valid, whether the workbook bytes match what was signed, and the original check results.
